author: Rajesh Sharma
text: Absolutely stunning property! The lobby is breathtaking with its grand architecture. The staff were incredibly attentive and went above and beyond to make our anniversary special. The Sea Lounge restaurant had amazing views and the food was world-class. Room service was prompt and the bed was extremely comfortable. Worth every penny for a luxury experience.

author: Priya Mehta
text: Beautiful heritage hotel with excellent service. The pool area is gorgeous and well-maintained. Breakfast buffet had an incredible variety. Only downside was the WiFi was a bit slow in certain areas of the hotel and the valet parking took longer than expected. Otherwise a fantastic stay.

author: Michael Chen
text: One of the finest hotels I've stayed at globally. The heritage wing rooms are beautifully decorated. The spa treatment was exceptional — best I've had in India. Staff remembered my preferences from a previous visit which was impressive. The Wasabi restaurant serves outstanding Japanese cuisine.

author: Anita Desai
text: The hotel has a lot of history and charm but is showing its age in some areas. Our room had minor maintenance issues — the bathroom faucet was dripping and the AC was noisy. The rates are quite steep for what you get compared to newer luxury hotels. However, the location facing the Gateway of India is unbeatable.

author: David Wilson
text: Impeccable service and a truly iconic hotel. The check-in process was smooth and the welcome drink was a nice touch. The gym is well-equipped but quite small for a hotel this size. The concierge was extremely helpful in arranging local tours. Would recommend for business travelers.

author: Sneha Patel
text: Disappointed for the price point. The room we got was on the smaller side and didn't have the sea view we expected despite booking a sea-view room. Had to be moved to a different room which took over an hour. The minibar prices were outrageous. The restaurant was overpriced and the food, while good, wasn't exceptional enough to justify the cost.

author: James Rodriguez
text: Pure luxury! From the moment you walk in, you feel like royalty. The attention to detail is phenomenal — fresh flowers in the room, personalized welcome note, and turndown service with chocolates. The rooftop bar has spectacular views of the Mumbai skyline. The in-room dining menu is extensive and everything we tried was delicious.

author: Kavita Nair
text: Stayed for a family celebration and the event team did a wonderful job arranging everything. The banquet hall is elegant and spacious. Kids loved the pool. The only negative was the noise from the nearby road could be heard in some rooms. The breakfast spread is one of the best I've seen in India.

author: Robert Kim
text: Historic and iconic but overrated in some aspects. The check-in queue was long despite having a reservation. The room was clean but the decor felt dated compared to modern luxury hotels. The bathroom amenities were premium though. Restaurant wait times were excessive during peak hours. The location saves it.

author: Meera Joshi
text: Third visit and it never disappoints! The loyalty program benefits are excellent. Got upgraded to a suite which was absolutely gorgeous. The butler service in the premium rooms is next level. The high tea at the Sea Lounge is a must-do Mumbai experience. Best hotel in India, hands down.

author: Arjun Kapoor
text: Incredible modern Indian cuisine! The menu changes seasonally which keeps things fresh and exciting. The cocktails are creative and perfectly complement the food. The jackfruit biryani was a revelation. Great ambiance with an industrial-chic vibe. Service was attentive without being intrusive. A must-visit for food lovers in Mumbai.

author: Sarah Thompson
text: Really enjoyed the innovative take on Indian classics. The small plates are perfect for sharing and trying multiple dishes. The pork vindaloo slider was amazing. Portions are on the smaller side for the price, but the quality makes up for it. The weekend brunch is excellent value. Reservations are a must — we waited 40 minutes without one.

author: Vikram Singh
text: Overhyped in my opinion. The food is good but not extraordinary for the prices they charge. The portions are too small — we ordered 6 dishes for 2 people and were still hungry. The noise level inside is very high making conversation difficult. However, the bar area is great for drinks and the staff is friendly.

author: Lisa Park
text: Best restaurant experience in Mumbai! The chef's tasting menu was outstanding — every course was a surprise. The presentation is Instagram-worthy. The wine pairing was thoughtfully curated. Staff were knowledgeable about every dish and its ingredients. The crab curry was the best I've ever tasted. Already planning my next visit.

author: Nisha Gupta
text: Creative menu with bold flavors. Love how they source ingredients locally and change the menu seasonally. The desserts are underrated — the mango shrikhand was divine. Only drawbacks: parking is limited and the restaurant can get very crowded and noisy on weekends. The outdoor seating area is lovely but limited.

author: Alex Martinez
text: Expected much more based on the hype and reviews. The food was good but nothing exceptional. The wait time for our table was 45 minutes despite a reservation. Our main course took over 30 minutes to arrive after ordering. The bill was shockingly high for the portion sizes. Would not return unless they improve the value for money.

author: Deepa Krishnan
text: A gem in Mumbai's food scene! The seasonal menu keeps it exciting every time we visit. The staff went out of their way to accommodate our dietary restrictions. The cocktails are innovative — the tamarind margarita is a must-try. Great place for celebrations and group dinners. The ambiance strikes the perfect balance between casual and upscale.

author: Tom Baker
text: Solid restaurant with a unique concept. The modern Indian fusion works really well for most dishes. The toddy shop prawns were incredible. Service was generally good but our server seemed overwhelmed during peak hours. The bar program is top-notch — some of the best cocktails in the city. Worth a visit if you're in Lower Parel.

author: Ravi Kumar
text: Best specialty coffee in Bangalore! The pour-over is exceptional and the baristas really know their craft. Love the minimalist decor and the natural lighting. The single-origin options change regularly which is exciting. Free WiFi works great for remote work. Only wish they had more food options — the menu is limited to basic snacks and pastries.

author: Emma Johnson
text: Great coffee shop for working or catching up with friends. The cold brew is smooth and strong. The space gets quite crowded during weekends and finding a seat can be challenging. Staff are knowledgeable about different brewing methods. The beans they sell to take home are also excellent quality. Prices are higher than regular cafes but justified for specialty coffee.

author: Amit Patel
text: Decent coffee but overpriced for what you get. A simple latte costs almost 300 rupees which is steep. The seating is uncomfortable for long work sessions. The music was too loud during my visit. The hazelnut croissant was stale. However, the actual coffee quality is genuinely good — the Ethiopian single origin was impressive.

author: Michelle Lee
text: My go-to coffee spot in Bangalore! The baristas remember regular customers and their preferences. The seasonal specials are always worth trying. The space is beautifully designed with plenty of natural light. They do coffee workshops on weekends which are incredibly informative. The subscription service for home delivery of beans is also fantastic.

author: Suresh Menon
text: Consistently good coffee and pleasant atmosphere. The V60 pour-over is my favorite. The outlet near the window is a great spot for laptop users. The iced mocha is indulgent but delicious. Wish they had better ventilation — it gets warm during afternoons. Parking is a nightmare in Koramangala but that's not really their fault.

author: Karen White
text: Way too hipster for my taste. The coffee is good but not worth the premium price. The staff seemed pretentious when I asked for sugar with my pour-over. The food options are severely limited — just a few pastries and sandwiches. The seating capacity is too small and there's always a wait. Not suitable for meetings or groups.

author: Pooja Reddy
text: Absolutely love this place! The attention to coffee quality is unmatched in Bangalore. Every cup is crafted with care. The new seasonal blend from Chikmagalur estates was phenomenal. The loyalty card is a nice touch. The staff are passionate about coffee and happy to explain the tasting notes. Best third-wave coffee experience in the city.
